channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Dozens of firms pull ads from Japanese network over sex scandal linked to its staff and celebrity
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Dozens of major Japanese companies have pulled their commercials on Fuji Television amid allegations company officials tried to cover up a sex scandal involving one of the country’s most famous TV hosts.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court declines to step into former GOP Rep. Steve King's meme fight
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to revive former Rep. Steve King’s (R-Iowa) bid to receive attorneys’ fees in a copyright fight that stemmed from a meme posted by his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: This house can protect itself during a wildfire
item.description: You've seen buildings and homes with fire sprinklers inside but what about on the roof? "It does save homes," said Harry Statter of Frontline Wildfire Defense, a company that makes an app and a system that can help defend your home from wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: California police arrest two in Palisades Fire evacuation zone after 'suspicious' behavior
item.description: Following arrests of alleged firefighter impersonators, officials say two more people have been taken into custody for using fake passes to unlawfully enter a fire evacuation zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump White House celebrates release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suspected settlers attacked Palestinian villages hours before Trump rescinded Biden sanctions
item.description: JINSAFUT, West Bank (AP) — Shortly after suspected Jewish settlers stormed Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank late Monday, setting cars and property ablaze, U.S. President Donald Trump canceled sanctions against Israelis accused of violence in the territory.
item.dc:creator: AREF TUFAHA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: More than 200 potential jurors summoned for trial of prosecutor in Ahmaud Arbery's death
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — More than 200 potential jurors were summoned Tuesday to a Georgia courthouse for questioning about whether they can serve impartially in the trial of a former prosecutor accused of meddling with police as they investigated the 2020 killing of Ahmaud Arbery.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Paxton sues Biden administration over ban on offshore drilling
item.description: During the Biden administration's final hours, Texas AG Ken Paxton filed his 106th lawsuit against the administration.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent was shot and killed in northern Vermont on Monday afternoon, the FBI said in a statement later that evening.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Dangerous winds return to Southern California as new wildfires break out
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Dangerous winds returned to Southern California on Tuesday as firefighters battled new fires amid dry conditions, and a pair of major blazes burned for a third week in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KRON4

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires rekindle 'eco-terror' arson suspect manhunt after fake firefighters arrested
item.description: Fugitive Josephine Sunshine Overaker's alleged eco-terror cell "The Family" has been linked to more than two dozen arson attacks from 1996 to 2001.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winds picking up in Southern California and at least 2 new fires break out
item.description: Winds picked up on Tuesday in Southern California and at least a couple of new wildfires broke out as firefighters remained on alert in extreme fire weather two weeks after two major blazes started that are still burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from the World Health Organization for the second time in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ohio State wins 1st national title since 2014, outlasting Notre Dame 34-23 in CFP championship game
item.description: Instead of crying over another collapse, Ohio State can celebrate another national title after holding off a Notre Dame comeback bid Monday night to walk away with a nailbiter of a 34-23 victory over the Fighting Irish.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Do US adults support Trump's Day 1 actions? Here's what polling shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's second term began with efforts to deliver on key campaign promises, including his vows to crack down on immigration and restore “ energy dominance.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes Laken Riley Act in first move after Trump inauguration
item.description: The Senate on Monday passed the Laken Riley Act, making the immigration-related bill the first piece of legislation to make it through the upper chamber in the new Congress and putting it a step closer to being signed into law by President Trump. Senators voted 64-35 on the bill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Biden endures painful but active last day of presidency
item.description: When President Biden walked through the Capitol on Monday with mere minutes left in his presidency, a reporter asked how he was feeling.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, WGN 9

item.title: Trump names Brian Driscoll to serve as acting FBI director after sudden Abbate retirement
item.description: Brian Driscoll, who has worked for the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) since 2007, has been named the acting director of the agency. The news came after Paul Abbate retired.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Planned Parenthood president, women's rights activist Cecile Richards has died at 67
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Cecile Richards, a national leader for abortion access and women’s rights who led Planned Parenthood for 12 tumultuous years, has died. She was 67. Richards died Monday at home in New York “surrounded by family and her ever-loyal dog, Ollie,” her family said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump returns to power after unprecedented political comeback, emboldened to reshape American institutions
item.description: “We won, we won, but now the work begins,” Trump said before a crowd of people in “Make America Great Again” hats.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners after Hamas frees 3 Israeli hostages
item.description: Israel has freed 90 Palestinian prisoners and detainees. The release came early Monday, more than seven hours after three Israeli hostages released from Hamas captivity in Gaza returned to Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Barkley runs for 78- and 62-yard TDs in the snow and Eagles top Rams 28-22 to head to NFC title game
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley blew through a hole and ran untouched in the wintry mix, the Eagles' 2,000-yard rusher smacking his helmet with his hand on his final snowy steps of a 78-yard touchdown run.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok restores service for US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok restored service to users in the United States on Sunday just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban, which President-elect Donald Trump said he would try to pause by executive order on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These lip balm ingredients may be making your chapped lips worse, experts say
item.description: It's tempting to reach for lip balm during the dry, winter months, but it may not be helping you at all.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Can you spot black ice? What to do if you start sliding or skidding
item.description: Black ice forms without creating bubbles, meaning it blends in with the road and becomes hard to detect.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KRON4

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters from the Navajo Nation worked tirelessly through a haze of dust to cut away dirt from a narrow road at the side of a mountain struck by a landslide in Southern California, coughing and sneezing amid the backbreaking work.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LOCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
